course_title: Exercise-based rehabilitation, strength and conditioning in musculoskeletal, sports and exercise physiotherapy

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the acute and long‑term physiological adaptations and maladaptations to different types of exercise across the neuromuscular, sensorimotor, cardiorespiratory, and metabolic systems.
Design individualised, safe and progressive evidence‑based exercise programs for diverse populations with musculoskeletal, occupational, sports, and chronic health conditions.
Select appropriate assessment, monitoring, periodisation, workload management and criteria‑driven progression to optimise health outcomes at individual and group levels.
Apply advanced clinical reasoning and behaviour‑change strategies to enhance engagement with exercise programs and support sustainable physical activity participation.
Evaluate and critically reflect on the effectiveness of exercise‑based rehabilitation to improve client outcomes and refine clinical practice.

assessments.task_description:
This online written examination assesses learning from modules 1, 2 and 3. This assessment task is to be completed online.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Students are required to design an exercise program for a patient / client returning from an injury to either sport or work.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
You will participate in an online oral examination (Viva Voce), where you will give a short presentation, respond to questions, and provide reflections on your PHTY7953 Exercise Program Written Assignment.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Assessment Procedure (95)

When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
PHTY7953 Online Webcasts

This weekly series of online online webcasts are designed for independent, self-paced learning. Each week includes 2-4 webcasts totalling 1-1.5 hour to complete. The webcasts provide information to enhance student's learning about exercise adaptations, prescription and programming for a range of populations. 


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 13(10 Aug - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Online drop-in sessions

Online drop in sessions are scheduled during the final week or week following each of the five modules in the course. The drop in sessions are an opportunity for students and ask questions of course staff for clarification of content or assessment.

The drop in sessions are scheduled at the following times:

Module 1: Thursday August 13th at 2pm QLD time (Semester week 3)

Module 2: Thursday September 3rd at 2pm QLD time (Semester week 6)

Module 3: Thursday September 17th at 2pm QLD time (Semester week 8)

Module 4: Thursday October 8th at 2pm QLD time (Semester week 10)

Module 5: Thursday October 29th at 2pm QLD time (Semester week 13)


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
